revision.text:
[[ja:大将軍神社 (京都市北区)]] {{Infobox religious building |名称 = Daishōgun Shrine |image = File:Taishogun-jinja.JPG |所在地 = {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}} {{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|en|Kita-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}} {{ill|Nishikamo|ja|西賀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikamo|1=Nishigamo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109360544}} {{ill|Kakurashacho|ja|角社町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakurashacho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}129 |位置 = {{Coord|35|03|31.0|N|135|44|32.4|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|qq=}} |祭神 = {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|ja|磐長姫命|1=Iwanaga-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}}
（originally {{ill|Daishōgun (Directional Deity)|ja|大将軍 (方位神)|lt=Daishōgun|lt_ja=大将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (Directional Deity)|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q167008}}） |神体 = |社格 = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |創建 = {{ill|609|en|609|qid=Q30897|lt=609|1=609}}（{{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} 17th year） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = Daishōgun神社
Nishikamo Daishōgun Shrine
Kawaraya-dera |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|October|en|October|qid=Q124|lt=October|1=October}}（portable shrine procession） |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Daishōgun Shrine|position=top|width=256|marksize=8|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q1154970|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.05861111111111|N|135.74233333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Iwanaga|qid=Q8191705|1=Iwanaga-hime}} }} '''Daishōgun Shrine'''' (Taishogun Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|en|Kita-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Nishikamo|ja|西賀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikamo|1=Nishigamo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109360544}}, {{ill|Kakurashacho|ja|角社町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakurashacho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kyoto. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Because several shrines titled {{ill|Daishōgun Shrine|ja|大将軍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Shrine|qq=}} or {{ill|Daishōgun Sha|ja|大将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大将軍神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大将軍神社|1=Daishōgun Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434525}} exist within the city, this shrine is sometimes distinguished by the name '''Nishikamo Daishōgun Shrine''' (Nishikamo Taishogun Jinja). ==Enshrined deities== ; Main Enshrined Deity * {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|ja|磐長姫命|1=Iwanaga-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} (reading: いわながひめのみこと) and the four familial deities are enshrined. In the Nishikamo area ({{ill|Imahara|ja|今原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imahara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Chinju-an|ja|鎮守庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju-an|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sōmon|ja|総門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Omiya Tajiri Town|ja|大宮田尻町|lt=Tajiri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiya Tajiri Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}){{refnest|group=Note|In the early modern period (prior to Meiji 22), this refers to the Nishikamo Village of Atago District, excluding the two hamlets of Kawakami and Hayashi|qq=}}, the deity is revered as the tutelary (ancestral) god of the local birthplace. == History == According to the inscription on the shrine’s information board, its foundation is dated to {{ill|609|en|609|qid=Q30897|lt=609|1=609}} (17th year of Suiko). During the construction of {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|qid=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}} under {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, shrines called “{{ill|Daishōgun Shrine|ja|大将軍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Shrine|qq=}}” were established around Kyoto to protect the royal castle, with this shrine designated as the guardian of the north.{{refnest|group=Note|It is also said to refer to the inner shrine of {{ill|Imamiya Shrine|en|Imamiya Shrine|qid=Q500955|lt=Imiya Shrine|1=Imamiya Shrine}} in Murasakino, Kita Ward, as well as Daishōgun Sha.|qq=}} In ancient times it was also known as '''Sumisha'''[https://web.archive.org/web/20100611022845/http://www.geocities.jp/sisekiguide/60/60walk.htm Kyoto History Walk – A Tour of Nishikamo’s Renowned Temples and the Land of Ushiwaka’s Birth] and as '''Kakusha''' (Sumiyashiro){{refnest|group=Note|The shrine’s address is “{{ill|Nishikamo Kakurashacho|ja|西賀茂角社町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikamo Kakurashacho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}”.|qq=}}, and was venerated as a deity for warding off epidemics and misfortune through the practice of katatagae (rearrangement). However, there are theories and traditions that suggest that “Kakusha” was a separate shrine from Daishōgun Shrine.『賀茂別雷神社境内諸郷の復元的研究』（{{ill|Suma Chie|ja|須磨千頴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suma Chie|1=Chikai Suma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24296751}}, Hōsei Daigaku Shuppankyoku, 2001）214 pages『西賀茂大将軍神社とその祭礼行事』（{{ill|Nakai Yukihiko|ja|中井幸比古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakai Yukihiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supervised by {{ill|Imahara Yosamaro|ja|今原嘉麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imahara Yosamaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tsūshobō, March 2016) pages 10–15, ISBN 978-4-902644-11-1 Also, from the time of its foundation, a tile kiln was established in the area, and during the Heian period it is said that tiles for the ancient government offices ({{ill|Government Office (Ancient)|ja|官衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government Office (Ancient)|1=Government Office (Ancient)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q104539349}}) were produced.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.kyoto.jp/bunshi/maibun/tizudaityou/kitaku.html|title=Registered Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, Natural Monuments, Cultural Property Conservation Areas, and Archaeological Site Inventory|publisher=Kyoto City|accessdate=2016-09-28|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20101203141642/http://www.city.kyoto.jp/bunshi/maibun/tizudaityou/kitaku.html|archivedate=2010-12-03|qq=}} Before the nearby {{ill|Jinkouin|ja|神光院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinkouin|1=Jinko-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588753}} was founded in the {{ill|Keien|ja|慶円|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keien|1=Kyōen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17216836}} era, its facilities were used as lodging for tile craftsmen who made offerings to the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|en|Kyoto Imperial Palace|qid=Q1202239|lt=Kyoto Imperial Palace|1=Kyoto Imperial Palace}}, and the location was known as {{ill|Kawaraya-dera|ja|瓦屋寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaraya-dera|1=Kawarayazen-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85882405}}.[http://eye.wakasa.jp/spot/detail/12/ Wakasa Seikatsu Kenkyūjo – Hitomi Kenkyūshitsu, Jinkouin] There is also a theory that “Kawaraya-dera” refers to {{ill|Shōju-ji|ja|正受寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōju-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which until the early Edo period was located to the south of Nishikamo Daishōgun Shrine,『西賀茂大将軍神社とその祭礼行事』（{{ill|Nakai Yukihiko|ja|中井幸比古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakai Yukihiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supervised by {{ill|Imahara Yosamaro|ja|今原嘉麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imahara Yosamaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tsūshobō, March 2016) pages 8–9, ISBN 978-4-902644-11-1 and that Shōju-ji, which had a close relationship with {{ill|Shōden-ji|ja|正伝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden-ji|1=Shōden Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545356}}, according to an old document (a temple diary from the Kōka era) of Shōden-ji, was more closely connected with Daishōgun Shrine than Shōju-ji during the Edo period.『西賀茂大将軍神社とその祭礼行事』（{{ill|Nakai Yukihiko|ja|中井幸比古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakai Yukihiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supervised by {{ill|Imahara Yosamaro|ja|今原嘉麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imahara Yosamaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tsūshobō, March 2016) pages 5–6, ISBN 978-4-902644-11-1 Needless to say, it also had a relationship with {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|上賀茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigamo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷神社}}. == Shrine facilities== [[Image:Taishogun-jinja2.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii]] [[Image:Taishogun-jinja3.JPG|thumb|250px|Western subsidiary shrine group]] The Main Hall was originally built in {{ill|1591|en|1591|qid=Q6766|lt=1591|1=1591}} (Tenshō 19) as the old main hall of the subsidiary ({{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=Sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}}) of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|qid=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} ({{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|上賀茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigamo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷神社}}) – known as the Kataka Shrine main hall – and was relocated between {{ill|1628|en|1628|qid=Q6840|lt=1628|1=1628}} (Kanei 5) and {{ill|1636|en|1636|qid=Q6885|lt=1636|1=1636}} (Kanei 13).{{refnest|group=Note|An inscription reading “Kamo Kataka Kandōtō Tenshō 19 – –” remains.|qq=}} It is built in the {{ill|Ikken-sha Nagaredzuri|ja|流造#構造|lt=Ikken-sha Nagaredzuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikken-sha Nagaredzuri|1=Nagare-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q210684}} style and is considered the oldest structure of the {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|qid=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}}. Together with an inscribed iron lantern from Kataka Shrine, it was designated a tangible cultural property by Kyoto City in {{ill|1985|en|1985|qid=Q2431|lt=1985|1=1985}} (Showa 60). == Sub-shrines== === Eastern subsidiary shrine group === * '''{{ill|Kakusha|ja|角社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakusha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' – enshrines {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (originally either {{ill|Daishōgun (Directional Deity)|ja|大将軍 (方位神)|lt=Daishōgun|lt_ja=大将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (Directional Deity)|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q167008}} or {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) * Kataka Shrine – {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Kotoshironushi) * {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|qid=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}} – enshrining {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|qid=Q644201|lt=Takao|1=Kuraokami}} (Takao) * Inari Shrine – enshrines {{ill|Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|宇気母智神|lt=Ukemochi|lt_ja=うけもちのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === Western subsidiary shrine group === * Atago Shrine – enshrines {{ill|Hinokagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具神|lt=Hinokagutsuchi|lt_ja=ほのかぐつちのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Matsuo Shrine – enshrines {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ooyamakui|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} * Hachiman Shrine – enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Kasuga Shrine – enshrines {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2564508}} * Sannō Shrine – enshrines {{ill|Ooyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|lt=Ooyamakui-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おおやまくいみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神|1=Oyamakui no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10935105}} == Festivals== * New Year Festival ({{ill|January 1|en|January 1|qid=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}}) * Spring Festival ({{ill|April 29|en|April 29|qid=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}})[http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~miyakonotukimairi/gyoji04/04taisyougunharu10.html Kyoto Monthly Visit – Daishōgun Shrine Spring Festival]{{dead link|date=2016th year 6月|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|qid=Q11261910|lt=Ooharae|1=Ōharae-shiki}} ({{ill|June 30|en|June 30|qid=Q2657|lt=June 30|1=June 30}}) * {{ill|Mushi-okuri|ja|虫送り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mushi-okuri|1=mushi okuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11241352}} (on {{ill|Midsummer Ox Day|en|Midsummer Ox Day|qid=Q16642915|lt=Doyō no Ushi no Hi|1=Midsummer Ox Day}}) * {{ill|Hassaku|ja|八朔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassaku|qq=}} ({{ill|September 1|en|September 1|qid=Q2859|lt=September 1|1=September 1}}) * Fire Burning Ceremony ({{ill|November 23|en|November 23|qid=Q3021|lt=November 23|1=November 23}})[http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~miyakonotukimairi/ohitaki/ohitaki00nisikamo07.html Kyoto Monthly Visit – Daishōgun Shrine Fire Ceremony]{{dead link|date=2016th year 6月|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|qid=Q11261910|lt=Ooharae|1=Ōharae-shiki}} ({{ill|December 31|en|December 31|qid=Q2912|lt=December 31|1=December 31}}) === Annual Festival === The shrine’s annual festival is held on a Sunday before approximately the 23rd of {{ill|October|en|October|qid=Q124|lt=October|1=October}}. On the preceding evening, a mochi pounding ceremony is conducted as an evening ritual. The festival procession features “Maiden” (primarily seven upper elementary school girls), along with “Representatives” (neighborhood association heads among parishioners), “Palace Visitors” (men responsible for guarding the maidens), “Child Priests” (mainly preschool children), and “Young Carrying Participants” (primarily elementary school students) all dressed in ancient ceremonial attire, parading lively through the parish. == 現地情報== ; Address * {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}} {{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|en|Kita-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}} {{ill|Nishikamo|ja|西賀茂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikamo|1=Nishigamo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109360544}} {{ill|Kakurashacho|ja|角社町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakurashacho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}129 ; Access * Nearest stop: Kyoto City Bus stop “Jinkouin-mae”; about a 10-minute walk (approximately 800 m to the south) ; Surroundings * Kichijoyama {{ill|Shōden-ji|ja|正伝寺|lt=Shodenji|lt_ja=正傳寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōden-ji|1=Shōden Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545356}} ({{ill|Kichijoyama Shōden Gokuzan Zenji|ja|正伝護国禪寺|lt=Kichijoyama Shoden Gokuzan Zenji|lt_ja=吉祥山正傳護國禪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kichijoyama Shōden Gokuzan Zenji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|qid=Q639625|lt=Rinzai school|1=Rinzai school}}, Nanzenji branch ({{ill|Nanzenji branch|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派|lt=Nanzenji branch|lt_ja=南禅寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanzenji branch|1=Nanzen-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q126509708}}) of the {{ill|Various Mountains|ja|諸山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Various Mountains|1=Shozan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632079}}. * Hoko-san {{ill|Jinkouin|ja|神光院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinkouin|1=Jinko-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588753}} – an independent temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon sect|1=Shingon Buddhism}}. * Nisshoyama {{ill|Hōun-ji (Kyoto City, Kita Ward)|ja|法雲寺 (京都市北区)|lt=Hounji|lt_ja=法雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōun-ji (Kyoto City, Kita Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo sect, Nishiyama Shinkusakha|ja|浄土宗西山深草派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo sect, Nishiyama Shinkusakha|1=Seizan-Fukakusa sect of Jōdo-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11556813}}.{{Cite web |title=Nisshoyama Hōun-ji – Official website of Hōun-ji in Kyoto’s Nishikamo area |url=http://houunji.com/ |access-date=2024-03-01 |language=ja|qq=}} This temple is distinct from the {{ill|Hōun-ji (Kyoto)|ja|法雲寺 (京都市)|lt=Hounji|lt_ja=法雲寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōun-ji (Kyoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (associated with {{ill|Kikuno Daimyōjin|ja|菊野大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuno Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) located in Kiyomizucho, Nakagyo Ward. * {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|上賀茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigamo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賀茂別雷神社}} * {{ill|Murasakino Daishōgun Sha|ja|紫野大将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasakino Daishōgun Sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 今宮神社 (京都市)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 今宮神社 (京都市)|1=Imamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q500955}} * {{ill|Joshin-in|ja|浄心院|lt=Joshin-in|lt_ja=淨心院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joshin-in|1=Joshin-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119379840}} – located in Omiya, {{ill|Yakushiyama Higashimachi, Omiya|ja|薬師山東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushiyama Higashimachi, Omiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; affiliated with the {{ill|Ōbaku|en|Ōbaku|qid=Q384728|lt=Ōbaku sect|1=Ōbaku}}. * Byakugōzan {{ill|Ichiyōin|ja|一様院|lt=Ichiyōin|lt_ja=一樣院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiyōin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – in Omiya Yakushiyama Higashi (within {{ill|Yakushiyama|ja|薬師山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushiyama|1=Yakushiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11622464}}); Ōbaku sect. * Jōdo Sect Jigen-ji (慈源寺) – located in Omiya Minamitajiri Town. * Jōdo Sect {{ill|Saikōji|ja|西方寺 (京都市北区)|lt=Saikōji|lt_ja=來迎山西方寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikōji|1=Saihō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11628329}} – located in Omiya Minamitajiri Town. * Seiryōzan (also known as Seiryōzan) {{ill|Reigenkōji|ja|霊源皇寺|lt=Reigenkōji|lt_ja=靈源皇寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigenkōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (with {{ill|Reigenji|ja|霊源寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigenji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊源寺 (品川区)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊源寺 (品川区)|1=Reigen-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97341920}}) – located in Nishikamo Kita Imaharacho; an independent temple of the Rinzai sect.{{Cite web |title=Facility Information｜Reigenkōji Cemetery – For Kyoto cemeteries |url=https://reigenkouji-boen.com/information/ |website=reigenkouji-boen.com |access-date=2024-03-01|qq=}} ** Tōseian * {{ill|Kawakami Daijingu-sha|ja|川上大神宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Daijingu-sha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Daijingu-sha, Kawakami Daijingu, or Kawakami Shrine){{Cite web |title=Kawakami Daijingu-sha Yasurai Festival 2024/4/14 (Schedule Details…) |url=https://kyototravel.info/kamikawayasuraimatsuri |website=Kyoto Guide |date=2011-04-09 |access-date=2024-03-01 |last=京太朗|qq=}} * {{ill|Myōkō-ji|ja|妙晃寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōkō-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in Nishikamo, Kawakami Town. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"|qq=}} === Sources === {{Reflist|qq=}} == External links == * [https://kanko.city.kyoto.lg.jp/detail.php?InforKindCode=10&ManageCode=29 Daishōgun Shrine (Komafuda) – Kyoto Sightseeing Navi] {{pref-stub-Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{神道 横|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = Daishōgun Shrine |image = [[File:Daishōgun Jinja (Nara) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Daishōgun Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} Shijō Ōji 3-chōme-4 |祭神 = {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|qid=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime|1=Iwanaga-hime}} |社格 = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=28|qq=}} |創建 = |本殿 = Constructed in the one-room Kasuga style with a “mise-tana” format{{sfn|奈良県史|p=910|qq=}} |例祭 = {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|qid=Q2921|lt=October 10|1=October 10}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 52.08 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 43.15 |地図 = Nara city }} '''Daishōgun Shrine'''' (Daishōgun Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located on Shijō Ōji 3-chōme in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It was relocated from the former {{ill|Saionji Village|ja|斎音寺村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{sfn|奈良市史|p=309|qq=}} == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|qid=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime|1=Iwanaga-hime}} == Shrine facilities== === Main Hall === There is a theory that the main hall is a relocated structure from the subsidiary of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|qid=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and the old main hall of {{ill|Enomoto Shrine|ja|榎本神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enomoto Shrine|qq=}}.{{sfn|奈良県史|p=910|qq=}} === Subsidiary Shrine === * Daijinja – the enshrined deity is {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}. It is located on the left (west) side of the main hall.{{sfn|奈良市史|p=309|qq=}} == Festivals== Although the rites are performed by a council of six, for the annual festival, priests are welcomed from {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|qid=Q118213|lt=Yamato-Koriyama|1=Yamatokōriyama}}’s {{ill|Yanagisawa Shrine|ja|柳沢神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagisawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{sfn|奈良市史|p=309|qq=}} == Sources == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == {{commonscat|Daishōgun Jinja (Nara)|qq=}} {{osm box|n|5300440888|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefecture History 8: Architecture|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 神社|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史|qq=}}|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Iwanaga Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|大将軍神社 (京都市北区)|version=100295626|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大将軍神社 (京都市北区)]] [[ja:大将軍神社]] [[ja:大将軍神社 (奈良市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11434527}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kita-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
